zh.Thornton, Leicestershire County, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾桑頓
zh.Maidstone, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特麥德斯同
zh.East Horndon, Essex, England: 英國艾可易亞斯特 霍登
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Tyrrell於大約1432年出生在英國艾可時，她的父親Sir Thomas Tyrrell29歲，母親Anne Marney21歲。她於1456年2月17日之前與Sir Robert Darcy在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1507年1月24日之前在英國，埋葬在英國艾可馬爾頓。

zh-hans.Thornton, Leicestershire County, England: 英国萊希斯特斯爾桑頓

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Tyrrell于大约1432年出生在英国艾可时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Tyrrell29岁，母亲Anne Marney21岁。她于1456年2月17日之前与Sir Robert Darcy在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1507年1月24日之前在英国，埋葬在英国艾可馬爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Tyrrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): perhaps a nickname from a derivative of Old French tirer ‘to pull’, in the same sense as French Tirand ‘one who pulls on the reins’, hence ‘obstinate, stubborn’. This name may have become confused with Turrell . A branch of this Anglo-Norman family has been in Ireland since the 12th century. Its name has been Gaelicized as Tirial.
English: habitational name from Tirril (Cumbria), which derives from Old Norse tyri ‘resinous wood’ + erg ‘shieling’.

variants.name: Terrel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Terrell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Tirrell
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Terrill
meanings: English (Cornwall; of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Turrell
meanings: most frequently an Anglo-Norman French pronunciation of Thorold (Old Norse Thóraldr, from the elements Thórr ‘Thor’ + valdr ‘power, rule’). This name and that in 2 may have become confused with Tyrrell .
from Middle English T(h)urild (Old Norse Thórhildr, Thorild, the latter a rare woman's name from the divine name Thórr ‘Thor’ + hildr ‘battle’).

variants.name: Tyrell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Trill
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived by the turnstile at the entrance to a village.
English (Sussex, also Kent): nickname, perhaps from Middle English tril(le) ‘turn, roll, whirl, spin’ (origin unknown, but perhaps Old English tyrl).

English (Sussex, also Kent) and Welsh: habitational name from one or more places named with Middle English tril(le) ‘turn, roll, whirl, spin’ (Old English tyrl), often used to describe moving water, such as Trill in Axminster (Devon), Tirle Brook (Gloucestershire and Worcestershire), and Trill Mill Brook (Oxfordshire).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Tyrrell was born about 1432, in Essex, England, her father, Sir Thomas Tyrrell, was 29 and her mother, Anne Marney, was 21. She married Sir Robert Darcy before 17 February 1456, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 24 January 1507, in England, and was buried in Maldon, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Tyrrell est née environ 1432, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Tyrrell, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Anne Marney, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert Darcy avant 17 février 1456, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 24 janvier 1507, à Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Maldon, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Tyrrell um 1432 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas Tyrrell 29 und ihre Mutter Anne Marney 21. Sie hat vor 17. Februar 1456 in Essex, England Sir Robert Darcy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 24. Januar 1507 in England und wurde in Maldon, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Tyrrell è nata il circa 1432 nel Essex, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas Tyrrell, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Anne Marney, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert Darcy prima del 17 febbraio 1456, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 24 gennaio 1507, nel England, e fu sepolta a Maldon, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Tyrrellが約1432年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Tyrrellは29歳，母Anne Marneyは21歳であった。1456年2月17日以前にイギリスエセックスでSir Robert Darcyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1507年1月24日以前にイギリスで死去し，イギリスエセックスマルドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Tyrrell(이)가 대략 1432년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Tyrrell(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Marney(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1456년 2월 17일 이전 영국 에세스에서 Sir Robert Darcy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1507년 1월 24일 이전 영국에서 사망했으며 영국 에세스 맬던에 묻혔다.

pt.1473: de 10 de janeiro de 1473 a 9 de janeiro de 1474
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Tyrrell nasceu aproximadamente 1432, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Tyrrell, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Anne Marney, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Robert Darcy antes 17 de fevereiro de 1456, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 24 de janeiro de 1507, em Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Tyrrell родилась около 1432 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Tyrrell было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Marney, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Darcy перед 17 февраль 1456 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 24 январь 1507 г. в местности Англия и была похоронена в местности малдон, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Tyrrell nació aproximadamente 1432, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas Tyrrell, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Anne Marney, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir Robert Darcy antes de 17 de febrero de 1456, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 24 de enero de 1507, en Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra.
